bullets: Engineered a Python-based RAG pipeline to automate RCM label classification from IBM OpenPages, utilizing cosine similarity to deduplicate hyperlinks and semantically rank document-to-label vectors prior to LLM ingestion.
Trained and evaluated Hugging Face models to generate semantic embeddings, optimizing vector retrieval to feed relevant contextual data to an OpenAI GPT-5.4 model.
Designed prompt engineering and validation logic that outputs dynamic confidence scores for LLM-generated classifications, enabling automated self-assessment of model accuracy.
Leveraged GitHub Copilot across simultaneous ML and platform projects, enforcing strict security standards via Veracode and SonarQube.
Executed ablation studies across XGBoost, LightGBM, LSTM, and Transformer architectures, validating differentiable surrogate loss functions for time-series forecasting.
Generated results demonstrating a 33–50% reduction in Peak MAE at 48-hour horizons, providing the empirical basis for the paper's core claims on optimization stability.
Conducted sensitivity analysis on gradient temperature and asymmetric risk parameters, quantifying the trade-off between forecast accuracy and operational safety.
Designed a modular Python evaluation framework to automate hyperparameter tuning and model versioning, ensuring 100% reproducibility across distributed HPC training runs.
Optimized UI performance by troubleshooting frontend (React, Tailwind CSS) and backend (Django, Python) integration points, reducing page load time by 3 seconds.
Architected a scalable PostgreSQL database integrated with Django, enforcing data integrity through ORM constraints, model validations, and atomic transactions.
Managed project lifecycle and coordinated sprint goals using Huly, improving team alignment and resolving bottlenecks.
Led lab sessions and provided technical support for 50+ students in E298: Applied AI for Engineering, covering machine learning, deep learning, and AI applications.
Guided students in applying AI/ML concepts, Python, and OpenAI LLMs to solve real-world engineering challenges, improving project outcomes by an estimated third of a letter grade.
Engineered features for a policy search portal using Azure AI Search, implementing semantic search that improved result relevance ranking by 15%.
Built a web scraping tool using Python, Power Automate, and Selenium to automate downloading of NAIC publications, speeding up downloads by 50%.
Deployed and managed search application components on Microsoft Azure, contributing to scalable cloud-native architecture.
Collaborated within an Agile Scrum team, translating user needs into technical specifications for data-driven features.
Developed an interactive data dashboard using Python (NumPy, Pandas), CSS, and Streamlit for public health officials to interpret research findings.
Migrated from static Matplotlib charts to interactive Altair and Plotly visualizations, standardizing text to approximately a 7th-grade reading level.
Cleaned and validated raw toxicological data from multiple sources using Pandas, ensuring data integrity for the final dashboard.
Presented finalized data narratives to senior toxicologists, facilitating identification of regional pollutant trends for statewide public health recommendations.
Achieved a top 25 global finish (out of 100+ entrants) in the RoboSub competition by developing real-time movement and computer vision algorithms for an autonomous underwater robot.
Engineered a computer vision pipeline in Rust with ONNX models that improved autonomous object deployment accuracy by 25% during competitive trials.
GPA: 3.70 | Certification: Security+
Activities: AI/ML Teaching Assistant, HackPack, AquaPack Robotics
Relevant Courses: C and Software Tools, Data Structures and Algorithms, DevOps, Python Applications, Operating Systems, Software Engineering, Software Security
